title: Issue-type vocabulary + containment graph
description: Formal shape of issue-types.yaml — the four GitHub-issue types (epic, feature, umbrella, task) the methodology recognises, what each represents, what each can contain via native sub-issues, what parent types each may sit under, and the textual first-line parent-ref form. Milestone is a separate primitive (see time-containers.yaml); sub-tasks are markdown checkboxes (see body-format.yaml). Other schemas reference entries here via the typed token `[issue-types:<id>]` (COR-019). Companion per COR-018.

types.description: Issue types. Keys are kebab-case ids; values match $defs.entry.

containment_invariants.description: Cross-cut invariants over the containment graph that aren't expressible as a single per-type field (e.g., 'EPIC does not contain EPIC', 'Feature does not nest'). Editorial; the engine's containment checks derive from the per-entry `can_contain` lists.

title_prefix.description: Bracketed title prefix without the brackets themselves (e.g., `EPIC`, `Feature`). The agent wraps in `[...]` when applying.

title_case.description: Casing of `title_prefix` when rendered. EPIC is `upper`; others are `title`.

structural_role.description: One-paragraph prose naming what this type represents in the hierarchy.

can_contain.description: Issue-type ids this type may contain via native sub-issues. Empty list means leaf (e.g., Task contains only markdown sub-tasks).

parent_issue_types.description: Issue-type ids that may sit immediately above this type. Empty list means top of the issue-type hierarchy (EPIC; its scheduling parent is a Milestone, a different substrate).

parent_ref_form.description: The textual first-line parent reference per MET-004 — the projection of the native sub-issue / Milestone link into the body. `<N>` is the placeholder for the parent's issue number.

parent_ref_optional.description: Whether the parent-ref first line may be omitted. True for EPIC when unscheduled (no Milestone yet); false otherwise.

has_body.description: Whether this type has a markdown issue body. True for all four issue types.

body_has_acceptance_criteria.description: Whether the body's minimum required structure includes a checkboxed acceptance-criteria section. Features and Tasks: yes. EPICs use 'Success criteria' (semantically distinct, separately encoded in body-format.yaml). Umbrellas: no — Umbrellas don't claim a capability.

gets_branch_and_pr.description: Whether this type produces a feature branch and a PR. Only Task. EPIC/Feature/Umbrella don't get branches; their closure is cascaded from children per MET-005.

issue_type_ref_bare.description: Bare id of another entry in this same schema's `types`. Used for in-schema lists (`can_contain`, `parent_issue_types`). Cross-schema consumers use `issue_type_ref` below.

issue_type_ref.description: Narrowed cross-schema reference token (COR-019) pointing at an entry in `issue-types.yaml`. Consuming schemas $ref this $def when a field's value identifies an issue type.
